# YouTube Upload

Upload finished videos to YouTube with metadata, thumbnails, and scheduling using the YouTube Data API v3.

## Execution Script

`execution/youtube_upload.py`

---

## Quick Start

```bash
# Upload with explicit metadata
python3 execution/youtube_upload.py \
  --video-id 20260303_ai_tools \
  --title "5 AI Tools That Will Replace Your Entire Tech Stack" \
  --description "In this video..." \
  --tags "ai,tools,tech" \
  --privacy private \
  --thumbnail .tmp/20260303_ai_tools/thumbnail.png \
  --category 28

# Upload from metadata.json (generated by metadata stage)
python3 execution/youtube_upload.py \
  --video-id 20260303_ai_tools \
  --from-metadata

# Generate metadata.json from pipeline state (no upload)
python3 execution/youtube_upload.py \
  --video-id 20260303_ai_tools \
  --generate-metadata

# Upload as unlisted for review
python3 execution/youtube_upload.py \
  --video-id 20260303_ai_tools \
  --from-metadata \
  --privacy unlisted
```

---

## Prerequisites

### 1. Enable YouTube Data API v3

1. Go to [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/)
2. Create a project (or select existing one used for Sheets)
3. Navigate to **APIs & Services > Library**
4. Search for "YouTube Data API v3" and enable it
5. Go to **APIs & Services > Credentials**
6. Create an **OAuth 2.0 Client ID** (Desktop application type)
7. Download the client secrets JSON

### 2. Configure Credentials

**Option A: Use existing credentials.json** (if already set up for Google Sheets)

The script will use the same `credentials.json` in the project root but will request YouTube-specific scopes. A separate `youtube_token.json` is stored so Sheets and YouTube tokens do not conflict.

**Option B: Set environment variables**

Add to `.env`:
```
YOUTUBE_CLIENT_ID=your_client_id_here
YOUTUBE_CLIENT_SECRET=your_client_secret_here
```

### 3. First-Time Auth

On the first run, a browser window opens for Google OAuth consent. You must:
1. Select the Google account that owns the YouTube channel
2. Grant permission for "YouTube upload" and "YouTube manage" scopes
3. The token is cached to `youtube_token.json` for future runs

---

## What It Does

1. **Authenticates** with YouTube Data API v3 (OAuth2 with token caching)
2. **Uploads** the video file using resumable upload (supports large files, auto-retry on server errors)
3. **Sets metadata** -- title, description, tags, category, language
4. **Uploads thumbnail** -- Custom thumbnail image (requires verified YouTube account)
5. **Adds to playlist** -- If playlist ID is specified
6. **Logs result** -- Records video ID, URL, and upload status to `upload_result.json` and `data/content_log.json`
7. **Updates state** -- Sets `youtube_video_id` and `youtube_url` in the pipeline state.json

---

## CLI Reference

| Argument | Required | Default | Description |
|----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `--video-id` | Yes | -- | Pipeline video ID (directory name under `.tmp/`) |
| `--from-metadata` | No | -- | Read all metadata from `.tmp/{video_id}/metadata.json` |
| `--generate-metadata` | No | -- | Generate metadata.json from state.json (no upload) |
| `--title` | No | From state | Video title (max 100 characters) |
| `--description` | No | "" | Video description (max 5000 characters) |
| `--tags` | No | [] | Comma-separated tags |
| `--privacy` | No | private | `public`, `unlisted`, or `private` |
| `--thumbnail` | No | Auto-detect | Path to thumbnail image |
| `--category` | No | 28 | YouTube category ID or name |
| `--schedule` | No | -- | ISO 8601 publish time |
| `--playlist` | No | -- | Playlist ID to add video to |

**Legacy compatibility** (for pipeline orchestrator):
| `--video` | No | Auto-detect | Direct path to video file |
| `--metadata` | No | -- | Direct path to metadata JSON file |
| `--visibility` | No | -- | Alias for `--privacy` |

---

## Metadata Format

The script reads and writes `.tmp/{video_id}/metadata.json`:

```json
{
  "title": "5 AI Tools That Will Replace Your Entire Tech Stack",
  "description": "In this video, we explore five AI tools that...\n\n--- Timestamps ---\n0:00 - Intro\n1:30 - Tool 1...",
  "tags": ["ai tools", "tech stack", "artificial intelligence"],
  "category": "28",
  "privacy": "private",
  "thumbnail": "thumbnail.png"
}
```

### Metadata Generation

Use `--generate-metadata` to auto-generate metadata from the pipeline state:

- **Title**: Extracted from `state.json` topic or explicit title field
- **Description**: Hook text + script summary + chapter timestamps from `parsed_script.json`
- **Tags**: Auto-generated from topic words, niche, two-word combinations, and common suffixes
- **Category**: From state or defaults to 28 (Science & Technology)
- **Thumbnail**: Auto-detected from video directory (`thumbnail.png`, `thumbnail.jpg`)

---

## Privacy Settings

| Setting | Behavior | Use Case |
|---------|----------|----------|
| `private` | Only you can see it | **Default.** Safe for review before publishing. |
| `unlisted` | Anyone with the link can see it | Share for review without making public. |
| `public` | Visible to everyone, appears in search | Final publish. |

**Safety:** The default is always `private`. Change to `public` only when you are ready to publish. The pipeline orchestrator uses `private` by default -- you can change visibility later via YouTube Studio or by re-uploading metadata.

---

## YouTube Category IDs

| ID | Category |
|----|----------|
| 1 | Film & Animation |
| 2 | Autos & Vehicles |
| 10 | Music |
| 15 | Pets & Animals |
| 17 | Sports |
| 20 | Gaming |
| 23 | Comedy |
| 24 | Entertainment |
| 25 | News & Politics |
| 26 | Howto & Style |
| 27 | Education |
| **28** | **Science & Technology** (default) |
| 29 | Nonprofits & Activism |

---

## Video File Detection

When `--video` is not specified, the script looks for video files in this order:
1. `.tmp/{video_id}/final.mp4` (post-production output)
2. `.tmp/{video_id}/assembled.mp4` (pre-enhancement output)

---

## Outputs

| Output | Format | Destination |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| Upload result | JSON | `.tmp/{video_id}/upload_result.json` |
| Upload result (legacy) | JSON | `.tmp/uploads/{slug}_result.json` |
| Content log | Appended JSON | `data/content_log.json` |
| Updated state | JSON | `.tmp/{video_id}/state.json` |
| Video URL | String | Logged to console |

---

## YouTube API Quotas

- **Daily quota**: 10,000 units
- **Video upload**: ~1,600 units per video
- **Thumbnail set**: ~50 units
- **Metadata update**: ~50 units
- **Practical limit**: ~6 uploads per day
- **Quota resets**: Midnight Pacific Time

---

## Error Handling

| Error | Cause | Resolution |
|-------|-------|------------|
| `No credentials found` | Missing credentials.json and no env vars | Follow Prerequisites setup |
| `Token refresh failed` | Expired or revoked token | Delete `youtube_token.json` and re-authenticate |
| `Server error (500/502/503)` | YouTube API transient error | Script auto-retries with exponential backoff (up to 5 retries) |
| `quotaExceeded` | Daily API quota hit | Wait until midnight PT for reset |
| `Thumbnail upload failed` | Unverified YouTube account | Verify your account at youtube.com/verify |
| `Video file not found` | No final.mp4 in video directory | Run post_production stage first, or use `--video` to specify path |

---

## Pipeline Integration

This script is called by `execution/pipeline_orchestrator.py` during the `uploading` stage:

```python
# In pipeline_orchestrator.py stage_uploading():
args = [
    "--video", str(video_path),
    "--metadata", str(metadata_path),
    "--visibility", state.get("visibility", "private"),
]
```

The orchestrator uses the legacy `--video` / `--metadata` / `--visibility` flags. Both CLI styles are fully supported.

---

## Pre-Upload Checklist

- [ ] Video exported in correct format (H.264, 1080p+)
- [ ] Metadata JSON generated (`--generate-metadata` or `generate_metadata.py`)
- [ ] Thumbnail image ready (1280x720, <2MB, JPG/PNG)
- [ ] Description includes timestamps
- [ ] Tags populated
- [ ] Category selected
- [ ] Privacy set to `private` for initial upload

---

## Edge Cases & Learnings

- **Upload fails mid-way**: Script uses resumable upload with 10 MB chunks and auto-retries on 5xx errors with exponential backoff.
- **Quota exceeded**: Wait until midnight Pacific time for reset. Consider scheduling uploads across days.
- **Thumbnail rejected**: Must be JPG/PNG, under 2MB, 1280x720 minimum. Account must be verified.
- **Duplicate title**: YouTube allows it, but avoid for SEO clarity.
- **Large files**: Resumable upload handles files of any size. Progress is printed per chunk.
- **Token stored separately**: YouTube uses `youtube_token.json`, not `token.json` (which is for Sheets). This avoids scope conflicts.
